title: ISSUE-01 — Define core data models
body:
**Epic:** Foundation
**User Story:** Supports all user stories
**Priority:** P0 — must be completed first
**Branch:** `feature/01-core-data-models`

## What to build

Create the following files, one type per file, in `NutriFacts/Models/`:

- `NutritionFacts.swift` — `struct NutritionFacts: Codable` with properties: `productName: String`, `isLiquid: Bool`, `macronutrients: Macronutrients`, `vitamins: [NutrientItem]`, `minerals: [NutrientItem]`, `allergens: [String]`, `ingredients: String?`
- `Macronutrients.swift` — `struct Macronutrients: Codable` with properties: `calories: Double`, `totalFat: Double`, `saturatedFat: Double`, `transFat: Double`, `carbohydrates: Double`, `sugar: Double`, `dietaryFiber: Double`, `protein: Double`
- `NutrientItem.swift` — `struct NutrientItem: Codable, Identifiable` with properties: `id: UUID`, `nutrientName: String`, `nutrientAmount: Double`, `nutrientUnit: String`
- `AppError.swift` — `enum AppError: Error` with cases: `productNotFound(followUpQuestions: [String])`, `aiUnavailable`, `networkError(Error)`, `invalidImage`, `parsingError`
- `AppState.swift` — `enum AppState` with cases: `idle`, `loading`, `success(NutritionFacts)`, `error(AppError)`

## Coding Convention Requirements

- All types use `let` for immutable properties
- `NutrientItem` uses `nutrientName`, `nutrientAmount`, `nutrientUnit` — not `name`, `amount`, `unit`
- All five types in separate files
- No UI code in this issue

## TDD — Tests to write first in `NutriFactsTests/Models/`

- `testNutritionFacts_isLiquid_isFalseByDefault`
- `testNutritionFacts_isLiquid_isTrueWhenSet`
- `testAppState_success_holdsNutritionFacts`
- `testAppState_error_holdsAppError`

## Done When

- [ ] All five files compile with no errors
- [ ] All four tests pass
- [ ] Zero SwiftLint warnings

title: ISSUE-02 — Set up NutritionViewModel and AIServiceProtocol
body:
**Epic:** Foundation
**User Story:** Supports all user stories
**Depends on:** ISSUE-01
**Priority:** P0
**Branch:** `feature/02-nutrition-viewmodel`

## What to build

Create the following files in `NutriFacts/Services/` and `NutriFacts/ViewModels/`:

- `AIServiceProtocol.swift` — `protocol AIServiceProtocol` with:
  - `func lookupNutrition(query: String) async throws -> NutritionFacts`
  - `func lookupNutrition(image: UIImage) async throws -> NutritionFacts`
- `NutritionViewModel.swift` — `@Observable final class NutritionViewModel` with:
  - `private(set) var appState: AppState = .idle`
  - `var searchText: String = "`
  - `var isRecording: Bool = false`
  - `private let aiService: AIServiceProtocol` — injected via `init(aiService: AIServiceProtocol)`
  - `func search() async`
  - `func clearSearch()`
  - `func analyzePhoto(_ selectedImage: UIImage) async`
  - Stub implementations only — no real AI calls yet

## Coding Convention Requirements

- `NutritionViewModel` depends on `AIServiceProtocol`, not a concrete type (Dependency Inversion)
- `appState` is `private(set)`
- All async functions marked `@MainActor`
- Each method does ONE thing (Single Responsibility)

## TDD — Tests to write first in `NutriFactsTests/ViewModels/`

- `testClearSearch_resetsSearchText`
- `testClearSearch_setsStateToIdle`
- `testSearch_setsStateToLoading`
- `testSearch_onSuccess_setsStateToSuccess`
- `testSearch_onError_setsStateToError`

## Done When

- [ ] `NutritionViewModel` and `AIServiceProtocol` compile
- [ ] All five tests pass using a mock `AIServiceProtocol`
- [ ] Zero SwiftLint warnings

title: ISSUE-03 — Build HomeView layout
body:
**Epic:** Text Search
**User Story:** US-01, US-02
**Depends on:** ISSUE-02
**Priority:** P0
**Branch:** `feature/03-home-view`

## What to build

Create the following files in `NutriFacts/Views/`:

- `HomeView.swift` — root view shown in `.idle` state with large navigation title, `SearchBarView`, suggestion chips, and empty state text
- `SearchBarView.swift` — contains `TextField`, clear button (visible when text non-empty), mic button, camera button

Suggestion chips: `["Apple", "Oat milk", "Greek yogurt", "Salmon", "Banana"]`. Tapping a chip sets `searchText` and calls `search()`.

## Coding Convention Requirements

- `HomeView` and `SearchBarView` in separate files
- Use `ThemeColor` for all colors — no hardcoded `Color(...)` values
- Both files must have `#Preview` macros
- `SearchBarView` receives closures for all actions — no direct ViewModel dependency inside `SearchBarView`

## TDD

- `testClearSearch_whenSearchTextIsEmpty_clearButtonIsHidden`
- `testSuggestionChip_onTap_setsSearchText`

## Done When

- [ ] `HomeView` and `SearchBarView` render correctly in preview
- [ ] Clear button appears/disappears based on `searchText`
- [ ] Tapping a suggestion chip fills the search bar
- [ ] Both views have `#Preview`
- [ ] Zero SwiftLint warnings

title: ISSUE-04 — Wire app state to navigation
body:
**Epic:** Foundation
**User Story:** US-01
**Depends on:** ISSUE-03
**Priority:** P0
**Branch:** `feature/04-app-state-navigation`

## What to build

- `LoadingView.swift` — centered `ProgressView` with contextual message "Looking up nutrition facts for [food name]…"
- Update `ContentView.swift` to use `NavigationStack` and switch on `NutritionViewModel.appState`:
  - `.idle` → `HomeView`
  - `.loading` → `LoadingView` overlay
  - `.success(let nutritionFacts)` → `ResultsView` (stub)
  - `.error(let appError)` → `ErrorView` (stub)

## Coding Convention Requirements

- `LoadingView` in its own file with `#Preview`
- No state management logic inside views
- `NutritionViewModel` passed via environment or constructor injection

## TDD

- `testAppState_loading_showsLoadingView`
- `testAppState_idle_showsHomeView`

## Done When

- [ ] Switching ViewModel state cycles through all four states
- [ ] `LoadingView` appears and disappears correctly
- [ ] Both new views have `#Preview`
- [ ] Zero SwiftLint warnings

title: ISSUE-05 — Build ResultsView shell with tab picker
body:
**Epic:** Nutrition Results
**User Story:** US-08, US-09, US-10
**Depends on:** ISSUE-04
**Priority:** P1
**Branch:** `feature/05-results-view-shell`

## What to build

- `ResultsView.swift` — accepts `NutritionFacts`, shows product name as nav title, segmented `Picker` with "Overview" and "Full Details" tabs, and unit label
- `UnitLabel.swift` — small reusable view showing "per 100g" or "per 100ml"
- `ResultsTab.swift` — `enum ResultsTab` for tab selection state

## Coding Convention Requirements

- Three separate files (one type per file)
- Tab selection as `@State private var selectedTab: ResultsTab`
- Both views have `#Preview` with mock `NutritionFacts` data
- No hardcoded colors

## TDD

- `testUnitLabel_isLiquidFalse_showsPer100g`
- `testUnitLabel_isLiquidTrue_showsPer100ml`

## Done When

- [ ] `ResultsView` renders with product name in nav bar
- [ ] Tab switching works
- [ ] Unit label shows correct text based on `isLiquid`
- [ ] All new types in separate files with `#Preview`
- [ ] Zero SwiftLint warnings

title: ISSUE-06 — Build Overview tab with pie chart
body:
**Epic:** Nutrition Results
**User Story:** US-08
**Depends on:** ISSUE-05
**Priority:** P1
**Branch:** `feature/06-overview-tab`

## What to build

- `OverviewTabView.swift` — donut/pie chart using Swift Charts (Carbs/Protein/Fat in grams), sweep animation, chart legend, 2×2 macro summary grid, `UnitLabel`
- `MacroSummaryCard.swift` — reusable card showing a value and label
- `ThemeColor.swift` — define `ThemeColor.carbohydrates` (orange), `ThemeColor.protein` (green), `ThemeColor.fat` (pink) if not already defined

Colors:
- Carbohydrates: `ThemeColor.carbohydrates`
- Protein: `ThemeColor.protein`
- Fat: `ThemeColor.fat`

## Coding Convention Requirements

- `OverviewTabView` and `MacroSummaryCard` in separate files, each with `#Preview`
- Use mock `Macronutrients` data in previews — no magic numbers inline in views
- All colors via `ThemeColor`

## TDD

- `testMacroSummaryCard_displaysCorrectValue`
- `testMacroSummaryCard_displaysCorrectLabel`

## Done When

- [ ] Chart renders with correct colors and segment labels using mock data
- [ ] Macro cards show correct values

title: ISSUE-07 — Build Full Details tab
body:
**Epic:** Nutrition Results
**User Story:** US-09
**Depends on:** ISSUE-05
**Priority:** P1
**Branch:** `feature/07-details-tab`

## What to build

- `DetailsTabView.swift` — `List` with `.insetGrouped` style, `UnitLabel`, all sections, disclaimer footnote
- Section views (each in own file):

  - `VitaminsSectionView.swift`
  - `MineralsSectionView.swift`
  - `AllergensSectionView.swift`
  - `IngredientsSectionView.swift`
- `NutrientRowView.swift` — reusable row: nutrient name left, value + unit right

Allergens section: shows "None detected" when allergens list is empty.
Ingredients section: shows plain text or "Not available".
Disclaimer text: comes from a constant, not hardcoded in the view.

## Coding Convention Requirements

- Each section view in its own file (one type per file)
- Each file has `#Preview` with mock data
- Indented sub-rows use `padding(.leading)`
- Disclaimer from a constant

- `testNutrientRowView_displaysNutrientAmount`
- `testAllergensSectionView_emptyAllergens_showsNoneDetected`

title: ISSUE-09 — Implement OpenAI text search service
body:
**Epic:** AI Engine
**User Story:** US-13
**Depends on:** ISSUE-02
**Priority:** P1
**Branch:** `feature/09-openai-text-service`

## What to build

- `Secrets.xcconfig` — at project root with `OPENAI_API_KEY = <your-key>`. Add to `.gitignore`. **Never commit the key.**
- `OpenAIService.swift` — conforms to `AIServiceProtocol`, implements `lookupNutrition(query:)`, calls OpenAI chat completions endpoint with `async/await`, reads API key from `Bundle.main.infoDictionary`
- `NutritionFactsResponseParser.swift` — `struct` responsible only for parsing raw API JSON into `NutritionFacts` or `AppError`

On unrecognized product: parse follow-up questions and throw `AppError.productNotFound(followUpQuestions:)`.

## Coding Convention Requirements

- `OpenAIService` and `NutritionFactsResponseParser` in separate files (Single Responsibility)
- No force unwrap, no force try, no force cast
- API key only via `.xcconfig` — never in source code
- All networking uses `async/await`
- `@MainActor` on ViewModel methods that update state

## TDD (use mock URLSession or `NetworkSession` protocol)

- `testLookupNutrition_validQuery_returnsNutritionFacts`
- `testLookupNutrition_unknownProduct_throwsProductNotFound`
- `testNutritionFactsResponseParser_validJSON_parsesProductName`
- `testNutritionFactsResponseParser_missingFields_throwsParsingError`

## Done When

- [ ] Typing "Apple" returns real nutrition facts from OpenAI (manual test)
- [ ] Unrecognized query returns follow-up questions (manual test)
- [ ] All four unit tests pass using mocks
- [ ] Zero SwiftLint warnings

title: ISSUE-10 — Implement connectivity detection and AI provider switching
body:
**Epic:** AI Engine
**User Story:** US-13, US-14
**Depends on:** ISSUE-09
**Priority:** P1
**Branch:** `feature/10-connectivity-ai-switching`

## What to build

- `ConnectivityMonitoring.swift` — `protocol ConnectivityMonitoring` with `var isOnline: Bool { get }`
- `ConnectivityMonitor.swift` — `final class ConnectivityMonitor: ConnectivityMonitoring` using `NWPathMonitor`, `private(set) var isOnline: Bool`
- `AIAgentService.swift` — `final class AIAgentService: AIServiceProtocol`, injected with `onlineService`, `offlineService`, and `connectivityMonitor`, delegates to appropriate service automatically
- `AppleIntelligenceService.swift` — stub that throws `AppError.aiUnavailable` (full implementation in ISSUE-14)

## Coding Convention Requirements

- Four separate files
- `AIAgentService` depends only on protocols (Dependency Inversion)
- `ConnectivityMonitor` uses `private(set)` for `isOnline`

## TDD

- `testAIAgentService_whenOnline_usesOnlineService`
- `testAIAgentService_whenOffline_usesOfflineService`
- `testAIAgentService_bothServicesFail_throwsAIUnavailable`

## Done When

- [ ] Turning off Wi-Fi uses `AppleIntelligenceService` automatically
- [ ] All three tests pass using mocks
- [ ] Zero SwiftLint warnings

title: ISSUE-11 — Add Info.plist permission entries
body:
**Epic:** Permissions
**User Story:** US-15, US-16
**Depends on:** ISSUE-03
**Priority:** P1
**Branch:** `feature/11-permissions`

## What to build

Add to `Info.plist`:
- `NSMicrophoneUsageDescription` — "NutriFacts needs microphone access to transcribe your spoken food name."
- `NSSpeechRecognitionUsageDescription` — "NutriFacts uses speech recognition to convert your voice into text."
- `NSCameraUsageDescription` — "NutriFacts uses your camera to identify food or read nutrition labels."
- `NSPhotoLibraryUsageDescription` — "NutriFacts accesses your photo library so you can choose a food photo."

Add inline permission-denied alerts in `HomeView` with "Go to Settings" button for each denied permission.

## Coding Convention Requirements

- Permission-denied alert text from string constants — not hardcoded inline
- No force unwrap when opening Settings URL

## Manual Test Checklist

- [ ] Microphone dialog appears on first mic tap
- [ ] Camera dialog appears on first camera tap
- [ ] Photo library dialog appears on first library access
- [ ] Denying any permission shows graceful inline message

## Done When

- [ ] All four `Info.plist` entries present
- [ ] Denying any permission does not crash the app
- [ ] Clear inline message shown on denial
- [ ] Zero SwiftLint warnings

title: ISSUE-12 — Implement speech dictation input
body:
**Epic:** Speech Input
**User Story:** US-03, US-04
**Depends on:** ISSUE-11
**Priority:** P1
**Branch:** `feature/12-speech-dictation`

## What to build

- `SpeechRecognitionService.swift` — `final class SpeechRecognitionService` using `SFSpeechRecognizer` with `en-US` locale
  - `func startRecording() async throws` — updates `transcribedText: String` in real time
  - `func stopRecording()`
- Update `NutritionViewModel`:
  - `func startDictation() async` — binds `transcribedText` to `searchText`
  - `func stopDictation()` — sets `isRecording = false`
- Update mic button in `SearchBarView`:
  - Active: red tint + pulse animation (respects Reduce Motion)
  - Inactive: `ThemeColor.accent` tint

## Coding Convention Requirements

- `SpeechRecognitionService` in its own file
- `async/await` only — no Combine or callbacks
- Locale: `Locale(identifier: "en-US")`
- Pulse animation via `withAnimation`

## TDD

- `testStartDictation_setsIsRecordingToTrue`
- `testStopDictation_setsIsRecordingToFalse`
- `testStartDictation_updatesSearchText`

## Done When

- [ ] Speaking fills the search bar with transcription
- [ ] Stopping recording leaves text ready to submit
- [ ] Mic button shows correct active/inactive state
- [ ] All three tests pass
- [ ] Zero SwiftLint warnings

title: ISSUE-13 — Implement photo input (camera + library)
body:
**Epic:** Photo Input
**User Story:** US-05, US-06
**Depends on:** ISSUE-11
**Priority:** P1
**Branch:** `feature/13-photo-input`

## What to build

- Update camera button to show `confirmationDialog` with "Take Photo" and "Choose from Library"
- `CameraPickerView.swift` — `struct CameraPickerView: UIViewControllerRepresentable` wrapping `UIImagePickerController` with `.camera` source
- On image selection: call `NutritionViewModel.analyzePhoto(_ selectedImage: UIImage)`
- `analyzePhoto` sets `appState = .loading` and calls `aiService.lookupNutrition(image:)` (stub — real vision in ISSUE-15)

## Coding Convention Requirements

- `CameraPickerView` in its own file
- No force unwrap on camera source check
- Image passed as `UIImage`
- Permission denial handled gracefully

## TDD

- `testAnalyzePhoto_setsStateToLoading`
- `testAnalyzePhoto_onSuccess_setsStateToSuccess`
- `testAnalyzePhoto_onError_setsStateToError`

## Done When

- [ ] User can take or pick a photo and app enters loading state
- [ ] Permission denial handled gracefully
- [ ] All three tests pass
- [ ] Zero SwiftLint warnings

title: ISSUE-14 — Implement Apple Intelligence offline service
body:
**Epic:** AI Engine
**User Story:** US-14
**Depends on:** ISSUE-10
**Priority:** P2
**Branch:** `feature/14-apple-intelligence-service`

## What to build

- Implement `AppleIntelligenceService.lookupNutrition(query:)` using Apple's `FoundationModels` framework with `@Generable` macro for structured output
- For photo input offline: use `VNRecognizeTextRequest` (label OCR) and `VNClassifyImageRequest` (food identification) via a separate `VisionAnalysisService.swift`
- If Apple Intelligence unavailable on device, throw `AppError.aiUnavailable`

## Coding Convention Requirements

- OCR/classification in `VisionAnalysisService.swift` (Single Responsibility)
- Graceful handling of `FoundationModels` unavailability
- No force unwrap on Vision results

## TDD

- `testAppleIntelligenceService_unavailable_throwsAIUnavailable`
- `testAppleIntelligenceService_validQuery_returnsNutritionFacts` (integration test — mark as slow)

## Done When

- [ ] With internet off, "Banana" returns nutrition data from on-device model (supported device)
- [ ] Unavailable model shows "AI unavailable" error message
- [ ] Tests pass
- [ ] Zero SwiftLint warnings

title: ISSUE-15 — Implement OpenAI photo/vision lookup
body:
**Epic:** Photo Input
**User Story:** US-05, US-06, US-07
**Depends on:** ISSUE-13, ISSUE-09
**Priority:** P2
**Branch:** `feature/15-openai-vision`

## What to build

- Implement `OpenAIService.lookupNutrition(image: UIImage) async throws -> NutritionFacts`:
  - Resize image to max 1024px on longest side before encoding
  - Base64-encode as JPEG and send as multimodal message to GPT-4o
  - Reuse `NutritionFactsResponseParser` from ISSUE-09
- `UIImage+Resize.swift` — extension with `func resized(toMaxDimension maxDimension: CGFloat) -> UIImage`

## Coding Convention Requirements

- Image resizing in `UIImage+Resize.swift` — not inside `OpenAIService` (Single Responsibility)
- No force unwrap on `UIImage` to `Data` conversion
- Base64 encoding as a single private method inside `OpenAIService`

## TDD

- `testUIImageResize_largeImage_reducesToMaxDimension`
- `testLookupNutrition_image_callsVisionEndpoint`
- `testLookupNutrition_image_validResponse_returnsNutritionFacts`

## Done When

- [ ] Photo of apple returns apple nutrition facts (manual test)
- [ ] Photo of nutrition label returns extracted normalized values (manual test)
- [ ] All three unit tests pass
- [ ] Zero SwiftLint warnings
